name: Metal Matrix Composites MMCs Laser Cleaning Dataset
description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for Metal Matrix Composites MMCs. Includes thermal, optical, mechanical, and laser interaction properties validated against industry standards.

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.description: Free to share and adapt with attribution

measurementTechnique: Laser ablation testing, material characterization, spectroscopy

includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Material Properties Database
includedInDataCatalog.description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for industrial applications

powerRange.description: Optimal average power for MMCs cleaning considering thermal conductivity and matrix integrity

wavelength.description: Near-IR wavelength providing good absorption in metallic matrix while minimizing damage to ceramic reinforcements

spotSize.description: Beam spot diameter balancing cleaning efficiency and spatial resolution

repetitionRate.description: High repetition rate for efficient cleaning while maintaining thermal management

energyDensity.description: Fluence level sufficient for contaminant removal without damaging MMC substrate

pulseWidth.description: Nanosecond pulse duration for controlled ablation without excessive thermal diffusion

scanSpeed.description: Optimal scanning speed for uniform cleaning coverage

passCount.description: Number of cleaning passes for thorough contaminant removal

overlapRatio.description: Beam overlap percentage ensuring complete coverage without excessive energy deposition

dwellTime.description: Effective interaction time per spot location

power.description: Typical range for industrial cleaning of MMCs at 1064 nm to remove contaminants without delaminating reinforcements; starting at 100 W for light contamination, max below 300 W to avoid substrate damage due to thermal sensitivity of composite interfaces (source: studies in Journal of Laser Applications, e.g., on Al/SiC MMCs).

scan_speed.description: Effective speeds for contamination removal on MMCs; higher for light oils/dusts (2000-3000 mm/s), lower for heavy oxides (500-1000 mm/s) to ensure ablation without excessive heat buildup; recommended 1500 mm/s for balanced efficiency (source: IPG Photonics laser cleaning manuals and industry standards for metallic composites).

pulse_width.description: Nanosecond regime preferred for MMCs at 1064 nm to achieve effective cleaning with minimal heat-affected zone (HAZ); picosecond (ps) or femtosecond for ultra-precision but costlier; 30 ns recommended to limit thermal diffusion in high-conductivity matrices like Al-based MMCs (source: laser physics research in Optics Express on pulsed ablation of composites).

repetition_rate.description: Typical for pulsed cleaning applications; 50 kHz balances speed and heat management for MMCs, as higher rates (>80 kHz) risk accumulation in lower-conductivity reinforcements like SiC, while lower aids heavy contamination (source: academic literature in Applied Surface Science on fiber laser cleaning of MMCs).

energy_density.description: Fluence range for safe contaminant ablation on MMCs; damage threshold ~2-4 J/cm² for matrices like Ti or Al with ceramic particles, lower to prevent cracking; 1.5 J/cm² recommended for effective cleaning without exceeding ablation thresholds (source: ablation threshold studies in Journal of Materials Processing Technology).

spot_size.description: Beam diameter range for cleaning; smaller for fine surface prep on MMCs (100-200 μm), larger for broad areas (300-500 μm); 200 μm recommended to focus energy on contaminants while minimizing exposure to heterogeneous MMC structure (source: equipment specs from Coherent and Trumpf laser systems for composites).

pass_count.description: Single pass for light contamination, multi-pass (2-4) for heavy layers to avoid thermal damage in MMCs; 2 passes recommended for uniform removal and surface preparation without risking delamination (source: industry case studies in Laser Cleaning Handbook and practical applications on aerospace MMCs).

overlap_ratio.description: Ensures uniform coverage in scan patterns; 60% typical for MMCs to prevent gaps in cleaning while avoiding overexposure that could heat sensitive interfaces; range adjusted for contamination density (source: process optimization studies in International Journal of Advanced Manufacturing Technology).
